context_passage: You are the head of a mutual fund, responsible for investing the available money in different funds/ securities. From the market trends you find that blue- chip companies and government stocks will generally not do as well as corporate bonds, in the coming year. However, government regulations require that at least one-third of your mutual fund’s capital must be in blue-chip stocks and another third in corporate bonds.
directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Under current regulations, what seems to be the best way for you to invest your funds so as to maximize profit?

options.label: a
options.text: two third government stocks, one-third blue- chip stocks

options.label: b
options.text: two third government. stocks, one-third corporate bonds

options.label: c
options.text: one third government. stocks, two-third corporate bonds

options.label: d
options.text: one third each of government stocks, blue-chip stocks and corporate bonds

explanation: Among all 4 options, only (d) is feasible as all other deviates the conditions given for multual fund portfolio.

context_passage: You are the head of a mutual fund, responsible for investing the available money in different funds/ securities. From the market trends you find that blue- chip companies and government stocks will generally not do as well as corporate bonds, in the coming year. However, government regulations require that at least one-third of your mutual fund’s capital must be in blue-chip stocks and another third in corporate bonds.
directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: If the return on government stocks suddenly goes up by 5%, you would probably:

options.label: a
options.text: sell blue-chip stocks to buy government stocks.

options.label: b
options.text: sell corporate bonds to buy government stocks.

options.label: c
options.text: sell both corporate bonds and blue-chip stocks to buy government stocks

options.label: d
options.text: no conclusive action can be taken

explanation: As blue chip is not giving good results as compared to corporate bond & so it is better to sell blue chips & buy government bonds. difference in the highest marks in Hindi between which of the following pair of years? (a) 1992 and 1995 (b) 1993 and 1995 (c) 1992 and 1996 (d) none of these

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: What is the number of apples plucked on day 6?
explanation: Do (1 + 2 + 3) + (4 + 5 + 6) in table 1 and then subtract (1 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5) from it. Therefore, the correct answer is option (b) 35 apples.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: For how many days, is it possible to determine the exact number of apples plucked by Munu?
explanation: We can find it out for all the days starting from day 1. See the solution of Q 3. For example for the 1st day: (1 + 2 + 3) + (4 + 5 + 6) from 1st table = 130 + 145 = 275. (2 + 3 + 4 + 5 + 6) from table 2 = 225 So, 1st day = 275 – 225 = 50 & similarly for any other case too we can find.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Disregarding table 2, how many of the following statements are definitely true? I. Number of apples plucked on day 2 is more than the number of apples plucked on day 5. II. Number of apples plucked on day 3 is more than the number of apples plucked on day 5. III. Sum of the apples plucked on day 1 and day 2 is less than the sum of the apples plucked on day 4 and day 5. IV. Sum of the apples plucked on day 2 and day 3 is less than the sum of the apples plucked on day 3 and day 4.
explanation: Only statement III is correct. To find the veracity of statement III, compare (1 + 2 + 3) with (3 + 4 + 5)

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: In the above question, how many of the statements can be verified for being right or wrong (using table 1 only)?
explanation: Only statement I and statement III can be verified.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Disregarding table 2; using how many of the following statements individually can we find the number of apples plucked each day by Munu? I. Total number of apples plucked from day 1 to day 10. II. Sum of the number of apples plucked on all the possible pairs of days. III. Difference in the number of apples plucked in between all the two consecutive days. IV. Sum of the number of apples plucked in all the four consecutive day periods.
explanation: Only statement II and III can give us the solution.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Disregarding table 1; using how many of the following statements individually can we find the number of apples plucked each day by Munu? I. Total number of apples plucked from day 1 to day 10. II. Sum of the number of apples plucked on all the possible pairs of days. III. Difference in the number of apples plucked in between all the two consecutive days. IV. Sum of the number of apples plucked in all the four consecutive day periods.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Company ‘Y’, however, cannot outrighthy reduce the price of its product because: I. of the company’s policy II. it lowers its image in the market III. it will reduce its profit
explanation: It is clearly given that company ‘Y’ cannot reduce the price as it lowers the image and reduces its profit also. Therefore, option (d) is the correct answer.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: The selling price of the product manufactured by company ‘X’ was low because: I. of less transportation costs II. they had less overheads as compared to company ‘Y’ III. the quantity per unit was less than that of their competitors
explanation: Transportation cost of company ‘X’ is comparatively less. Besides being a new entrant, it has kept its overhead expenses low. Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Why did company ‘Y’ go for flexi-packs? I. to be in tune with marketing trends II. easy to handle on filling lines III. to save on packaging costs
explanation: Flexi-packs reduce the cost of packaging. Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Savings made in cost of packaging I. helped reduce cost of production II. helped reduce selling price III. allow more production
explanation: The savings made can be trickled down to the customers by way of reducing the price of the product. Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Why was company ‘Y’ selling the same type of product at a rate higher than that of company ‘X’? (I) Before company ‘X’ entered the market, company ‘Y’ enjoyed a monopoly in the market and took advantage of the same. (II) As company ‘Y’ had a long standing, its overheads were comparatively higher. (III) Their total cost of production was higher.
explanation: Neither statement I nor II can be verified. However, statement III is mentioned very clearly. Therefore, option (b) is the correct answer.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: The market survey conducted by company ‘Y’ revealed that their product was a quick consumption item of a higher quality with a longer shelf-life. What was the conclusion drawn from the survey? I. Being a quick consumption item, it can have a cheaper packaging, as the problem of aging will not be there. II. Longer shelf-life shows that the product has durability and therefore can stand for a longer period in the market without changes in the quality of the product. III. Their product enjoyed better reputation than that of company ‘X’.
explanation: Only statement I and II can be verified. It is given that quality of product ‘Y’ was better than all others, but it cannot be concluded from here that it enjoyed better reputation than company ‘X’. Therefore, option (c) is the correct answer.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: If it is mandated that a student who wishes to enroll for BM or CB has to take MR, then which of the followings statements is necessarily true?

options.label: a
options.text: In 1999, only one student must have enrolled for both BM and CB.

options.label: b
options.text: In 2001, not more than 7 students could have enrolled both for BM and CB.

options.label: c
options.text: In 2000, at least 9 students must have enrolled for both BM and CB.

options.label: d
options.text: In 2002, not more than 5 students could have enrolled both for BM and CB.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: It is mandated that those who enroll for IM should take SM or CB. Then, which of the statements is necessarily true?

options.label: a
options.text: In 1999, the number of students who enrolled for SM, CB and IM is at least 4.

options.label: b
options.text: In 2000, the number of students who enrolled for IM and CB and not SN is not more than 10.

options.label: c
options.text: If at least four students who took IM and SM also took CB, then the maximum number of students who took CB but not IM is 17.

options.label: d
options.text: In 2002, the number of students who enrolled for SM, CB and IM is at least 3.

explanation: In 1999, difference of students in IM and SM = 29 – 25 In SM and CB = 25 – 21 = 4 Hence, at least 4 students enrolled for SM, CB and IM.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: If the rules of this school prescribe that a student should take 3 and not more than 3 marketing area courses to specialize in the marketing stream, which of the following statements cannot be true?

options.label: a
options.text: In 1999, all those who took CB also took BM and MR.

options.label: b
options.text: In 2000, all those who took BM also took SM and IM.

options.label: c
options.text: In 2001, all those who took BM also took SM and IM.

explanation: Since, in 2001, number of students in BM > number of students in SM

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: On 19th June how much Yen would ₹8000 amount to?
explanation: ₹8000 can be changed to Yen by 1st converting ₹ to Dollar & than Dollar to Yen 46 ₹ = 1 $ 1 8000 ₹ = $ 46 1 $ = 106.75 Yen 1 8000 ₹ = × 8000 × 106.75 Yen = 18600 Yen 46

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: If the Dollar/Yen ratio follows the same trend from 24th to 27th as from 21st to 24th, then what will be the value of $ 50 on 27th June?
explanation: If Dollar/Yen ratio follows same trend from 24th to 27th, then Dollar/Yen = 108 So, 1 $ = 108 Yen 50 $ = 50 × 108 Yen = 5400 Yen

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: A jacket costs $ 400. Mohan has ₹18,200, £ 225 and ¥ 35000 with him. If he can use only one currency to buy the jacket, he would be able to buy the jacket on 20th June using:
explanation: Converting all amounts to Dollar 18200 ₹ = 18200/45.5 $ = 400 $ 225 £ = 225 × 1.6 $ = 360 $ 35,000 Yen = 35000/106.25 $ = 329.4 $ So, ₹18,200 can be used to buy the Jacket.

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: On 15th June the Dollar/Rupee ratio was 46.5. On that day ₹5000 would fetch_________ Pounds.
explanation: We can convert ₹ to $ but not in £. We need ratio for ₹ & £ or $ & £ to solve it. So, it cannot be determined. 52 + 66 + 64 + 75 + 58 315

explanation: The difference is 9. Therefore the correct answer is option (a) 55 – 46

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: What was the approximate percentage increase in average marks in history from 1992 and 1993?
explanation: Percentage Increase = × 100 = 20 % 46

question_text: The average highest marks in English in 1992, 1993 and 1996 were exactly equal to the highest marks in Hindi in which of the following years?
explanation: Average highest marks 85 + 80 + 75 240 = = 80 3 3 Therefore the correct answer is option (d)
